aṅgārakāsu.meaning: a pit of charcoal; a pit of burning embers

breakdown: Negative prefix meaning “without” (na > an before a vowel).

aṇaṇa free from.note: uncertain: this headword is a glossary-row artifact; the entry is aṇaṇa with the gloss “free from debt”. See aṇaṇa.

aṇukathūlā.meaning: subtle and gross; fine and coarse

aṇumatto.meaning: even the smallest; of the least measure
aṇumatto.gram: adjective, nominative singular masculine, compound (aṇu + matta)
breakdown: Measuring, of the amount of; from matta “measure”.

aṇṇava ocean atikkama.meaning: aṇṇava: ocean; atikkama: transgressing, going beyond, surpassing
aṇṇava ocean atikkama.note: uncertain: this headword is a glossary-row artifact combining two separate entries, aṇṇava (“ocean”) and atikkama (“transgressing, surpassing”).

aṭṭha-purisa-puggalā.meaning: the eight kinds of individuals; the eight noble persons
aṭṭha-purisa-puggalā.summary: This phrase names the four pairs of men — the eight noble individuals on the four stages of the path and their fruits — praised in the recollection of the Saṅgha.

breakdown: The eighth (accusative; the m bridges in sandhi).
They take, they undertake; from ādiyati.

aṭṭhamī ca pakkhassa.meaning: and the eighth (day) of the fortnight (with the 14th and 15th, the uposatha days)
breakdown: Of the fortnight; genitive of pakkha.

aṭṭhapanā.gram: feminine noun (ā + ṭhapanā, from ṭhapeti “to place”)
breakdown: Prefix (ā, shortened), with sandhi doubling of the ṭ.
Placing, arranging; from ṭhapeti, causative of tiṭṭhati.

aṭṭhaṅgikamāhuposathaṁ.meaning: they call it the uposatha with its eight observances; the eightfold uposatha
breakdown: They say, they call (the m bridges in sandhi).
The uposatha, the observance day with its precepts.

aṭṭīyati.meaning: to be worried, distressed; to be troubled
aṭṭīyati.gram: verb, present tense, 3rd person singular (denominative from aṭṭa “distress”)

aṭṭīyitvā.meaning: having been worried; having become distressed

aṭṭīyitvā/ aṭṭiyitvā.meaning: having been worried; having become distressed
aṭṭīyitvā/ aṭṭiyitvā.note: uncertain: this headword is a glossary-row artifact listing both spellings of the absolutive; see aṭṭīyitvā.

bahinikkhantāvasesassa.meaning: of what remains after going outside; of the remainder left outside
breakdown: Departed, gone out; past participle of nikkhamati.
Of the remainder; genitive of avasesa (the vowels coalesce to -ntāva-).

bahujanahitasuttavaṇṇanā..meaning: the commentary on the Bahujanahita Sutta (the discourse “For the Benefit of Many People”)

bahujanahitasuttaṁ.meaning: the Bahujanahita Sutta, the discourse “For the Benefit of Many People” (bahu + jana + hita)

bahujanahitāya.meaning: for the benefit of many people

bahujanasukhāya.meaning: for the happiness of many people

bahulamanuvitakketi.meaning: thinks much upon; reflects on repeatedly
bahulamanuvitakketi.gram: verb, present tense, 3rd person singular, compound (bahulaṁ + anuvitakketi)
breakdown: Much, manyfold (adverb; the m bridges in sandhi).
Thinks over, reflects upon; anu + vi + √takk.

bahulīkarissati.meaning: will make much of; will practise frequently, will develop
bahulīkarissati.gram: verb, future 3rd person singular, compound (bahulī + karoti)
breakdown: Will make, will do; future of karoti.

bahulīkatā.meaning: made much of; practised frequently, developed
bahulīkatā.gram: past participle of bahulīkaroti, compound verb
breakdown: Made, done; past participle of karoti.

bahussuto.meaning: of great learning; well versed (literally “having heard much”)
bahussuto.gram: adjective, nominative singular masculine, compound (bahu + suta)
breakdown: Much, many (with sandhi doubling of the following s).
Heard; past participle of suṇāti; figuratively, learned.

bahutarāni.gram: adjective, comparative of bahu, neuter plural (bahu + -tara)

bahūpakāro.meaning: of much benefit; very helpful, of great service
bahūpakāro.gram: adjective, nominative singular masculine, compound (bahu + upakāra)
breakdown: Many, much (bahu + upa- coalesces to bahūpa-).

bajjhati.meaning: to be bound; to be imprisoned
bajjhati.gram: verb, present tense, 3rd person singular, passive of bandhati (√bandh)

balakāyasmiṁ.gram: masculine noun, locative singular, compound (bala + kāya)
breakdown: Body, mass, group; locative singular — balakāya “the body of troops, army”.

balavacittekaggatā.meaning: strong one-pointedness of mind; firm unification of the mind
breakdown: One-pointedness; eka “one” + gata “gone” + -tā: the state of being gone to one point.

balibaddassa.meaning: of an ox yoked to the plough

baliṁ.gram: masculine noun, accusative singular of bali

bandhanā.gram: neuter noun (√bandh + ana), here in the ablative sense “from bondage”

bandhanāgāriko.meaning: prison warden; jailer (literally “the man of the house of bonds”)
breakdown: Suffix: belonging to, in charge of.

bavhābādhasaṁvattanikā.meaning: conducive to much sickness; leading to bad health
breakdown: Of much sickness, in bad health (bahu “many” + ābādha “disease”, with bahu > bavh- before the vowel).

bavhābādhasaṁvat­.meaning: conducive to much sickness; leading to bad health
bavhābādhasaṁvat­.note: uncertain: this headword is a line-break fragment of bavhābādhasaṁvattanikā in the source vocabulary; see that entry.

bavhābādho.meaning: having many diseases; of bad health
bavhābādho.gram: adjective, nominative singular masculine, compound (bahu + ābādha)
breakdown: Many (bahu > bavh- before the following vowel).

become.meaning: become (English gloss fragment from the source glossary)
become.note: uncertain: this headword is an English glossary-row artifact, not a Pāli word; the source row gives no Pāli headword.

breakdown: Root “to be, become” (bhav > bhab before the suffix).

bhabbarūpatāya.meaning: by the appearance of likelihood; through seeming capable
bhabbarūpatāya.gram: feminine abstract noun, instrumental singular, compound (bhabba + rūpa + -tā)
breakdown: Abstract suffix -tā in the instrumental singular.
Stem of bhajati “to associate with, resort to”.

bhajate.gram: verb, present tense, 3rd person singular, middle voice, of bhajati (√bhaj)

bhajatha.meaning: cultivate the acquaintance of; be devoted to, follow (you all)
bhajatha.gram: verb, present/imperative 2nd person plural of bhajati (√bhaj)

bhaje.meaning: one should keep company with; one should associate with, resort to
bhaje.gram: verb, optative/middle 3rd person singular of bhajati (√bhaj)

bhajitabbo.meaning: to be associated with; whose acquaintance should be cultivated, to be embraced
bhajitabbo.gram: potential participle, nominative singular masculine, of bhajati (√bhaj + itabba)
